description_long: This is a gray, Soviet-era military jet aircraft with a streamlined fuselage and swept-back wings, featuring prominent red star insignias on the wings and tail. The nose is rounded and houses an air intake, while the cockpit is positioned just behind the nose with a clear canopy. The aircraft has a single vertical stabilizer and a conventional tailplane, with the main landing gear extending from the underside of the wings and a nose wheel under the front fuselage. The wings are low-mounted and swept, giving the jet a distinctive, aggressive profile. The surface appears metallic with a matte finish, and the aircraft is marked with the number '09' in red on the forward fuselage. This type of airplane is typically used for military operations and is not commonly found in urban environments except as a display or in transit.
description: A gray, swept-wing Soviet military jet with red star insignias, a rounded nose intake, and a forward cockpit under a clear canopy.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the rounded nose intake, forward cockpit, and left wing with red star insignia.
description_view_1: Top-down left view highlighting the swept-back wings, tail section, and both wing insignias.
description_view_2: Top-down right view displaying the right wing, tailplane, and the aircraft's streamlined fuselage.
description_view_3: Front-right view focusing on the nose intake, cockpit canopy, and right wing with red star insignia.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single jet engine integrated in the fuselage nose intake.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose wheel, all retractable.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
